title: The Global Ebola Response
subtitle: From a local outbreak to an international public-health test
description: A guided reading path for understanding how an Ebola outbreak can move from local hospitals to border controls, diplomacy, community trust, and global emergency planning.
cadence: Updated when the story has a meaningful new development.

articles.label: Part 1
articles.note: How the outbreak began and why cross-border spread made it more serious.

articles.label: Part 2
articles.note: Why the WHO emergency declaration changed the scale of the response.

articles.label: Part 3
articles.note: How conflict and mistrust made disease control harder.

articles.label: Part 4
articles.note: How quarantine policy became a legal and diplomatic issue.

articles.label: Part 5
articles.note: How global health agencies tried to coordinate a wider containment plan.
